key_events: Protagonist Kim Dok-Ja, a 28-year-old contract employee, encounters his colleague Yoo Sang-Ah on the subway during his commute.
Dok-Ja's favorite webnovel, 'Three Ways to Survive in a Ruined World' (Ways of Survival), concludes today after 10 years and 3149 chapters, with Dok-Ja being the sole consistent reader.
The novel's author, 'tls123', sends Dok-Ja a private message, expressing deep gratitude for his readership, informing him of the novel's monetization at 7 PM, and offering a 'special gift' via email.
Dok-Ja receives an email from 'tls123' confirming the monetization and wishing him good luck, then notices the novel has been deleted from the platform right before 7 PM.
At precisely 7 PM, the subway experiences a sudden, chaotic event, accompanied by an announcement: 'The free service of Planetary System 8612 has ended. The Main Scenario begins now...'
character_updates: Kim Dok-Ja is introduced as a 28-year-old contract worker with a third-rate university background, whose hobby is reading webnovels. He perceives his life as a 'reader' (Dok-Ja means 'reader' and 'only child'), lacking the 'protagonist' qualities he attributes to others like Yoo Sang-Ah. He feels a deep connection to 'Ways of Survival' as his personal escape. Yoo Sang-Ah is portrayed as a popular, dedicated, and capable colleague who has been promoted to a full-time position, actively studying Spanish on her commute. She represents the 'protagonist' archetype in Dok-Ja's eyes. The author 'tls123' is revealed to be a real person who has been writing 'Ways of Survival' for over a decade, seemingly for Dok-Ja alone, and initiates the shift in Dok-Ja's reality.
lore_and_worldbuilding: The central piece of lore is the webnovel 'Three Ways to Survive in a Ruined World,' a massive fantasy epic of 3149 chapters, read by Dok-Ja for over 10 years. Its ending triggers the start of a world-altering event. The mention of 'Planetary System 8612' and its 'free service' ending, followed by the 'Main Scenario' beginning, strongly implies a sudden shift from a normal reality to a system-driven, game-like world or apocalyptic scenario.
ending_cliffhanger: The chapter ends abruptly at 7 PM with a global system announcement: 'The free service of Planetary System 8612 has ended. The Main Scenario begins now...' This is accompanied by sudden chaos on the subway and Dok-Ja's realization that 'the genre of his life changed' at that exact moment, leaving readers wondering what 'Main Scenario' entails and how Dok-Ja's life as a 'reader' of an apocalyptic novel will play out in a newly ruined world.
